user_prompt: Our privacy policy is 2 years old. Generate a checklist of what to update for current GDPR + ePrivacy.
actual_output:
## Privacy Policy Update Checklist — GDPR + ePrivacy

*Disclaimer: This is compliance guidance, not legal advice. Have updated policy reviewed by qualified legal counsel before publishing.*

### Section 1: Mandatory GDPR Disclosures — Art. 13/14

#### Identity & Contact
- [ ] **Controller identity** — Full legal entity name, registered address
- [ ] **Controller contact details** — Email, postal address for privacy inquiries
- [ ] **DPO contact** (if appointed) — Art. 13(1)(b)
- [ ] **EU representative** (if controller is outside EU) — Art. 27

#### Processing Details
- [ ] **Purposes of processing** — Listed per processing activity, not generic
- [ ] **Lawful basis per purpose** — Art. 6(1)(a-f); special category basis under Art. 9 if applicable
- [ ] **Legitimate interests explained** — Where Art. 6(1)(f) is used, state the specific interest
- [ ] **Categories of personal data** (if collected indirectly) — Art. 14(1)(d)
- [ ] **Source of data** (if not from data subject) — Art. 14(2)(f)

#### Recipients & Transfers
- [ ] **Categories of recipients** — Processors, sub-processors, third parties
- [ ] **Named third parties** where required (e.g., analytics, payment processors)
- [ ] **International transfers disclosed** — Art. 13(1)(f)
- [ ] **Transfer safeguards specified** — SCCs, adequacy decision, BCRs
- [ ] **Post-Schrems II update** — If transferring to US, reference EU-US Data Privacy Framework (2023) or supplementary measures

#### Retention & Rights
- [ ] **Retention periods per data category** — Specific durations or criteria, not "as long as necessary"
- [ ] **Data subject rights enumerated:**
  - [ ] Right of access (Art. 15)
  - [ ] Right to rectification (Art. 16)
  - [ ] Right to erasure (Art. 17)
  - [ ] Right to restriction (Art. 18)
  - [ ] Right to data portability (Art. 20)
  - [ ] Right to object (Art. 21)
  - [ ] Rights related to automated decision-making (Art. 22)
- [ ] **Right to withdraw consent** — Where consent is the basis (Art. 7(3))
- [ ] **Right to lodge complaint with supervisory authority** — Art. 13(2)(d)
- [ ] **How to exercise rights** — Specific contact method, response timeline (1 month)

#### Automated Decision-Making
- [ ] **Existence of automated decisions/profiling disclosed** — Art. 13(2)(f)
- [ ] **Logic, significance, consequences explained** if applicable

### Section 2: ePrivacy / Cookie Updates

- [ ] **Cookie banner aligned with EDPB 2023 guidelines:**
  - [ ] "Reject All" button equally prominent as "Accept All"
  - [ ] No pre-ticked boxes (CJEU *Planet49*)
  - [ ] No "consent walls" forcing acceptance
  - [ ] Granular category controls (necessary, analytics, marketing)
- [ ] **Cookie policy includes:**
  - [ ] Each cookie named with purpose, duration, third party
  - [ ] Distinction between strictly necessary vs. consent-required
  - [ ] How to withdraw consent (as easy as giving it)
- [ ] **Tracking technologies beyond cookies covered** — Pixels, fingerprinting, SDKs

### Section 3: Recent Regulatory Developments (2022-2024)

- [ ] **Schrems II compliance** — Transfer Impact Assessments referenced for non-adequate countries
- [ ] **EU-US Data Privacy Framework** — If using DPF-certified US processors (replaces Privacy Shield)
- [ ] **Dark patterns avoided** — EDPB Guidelines 03/2022 on deceptive design
- [ ] **Children's data** — Age verification mechanisms; parental consent for under-16 (or local age threshold, 13-16 varies by member state) — Art. 8
- [ ] **Data breach notification commitment** — Reference 72-hour authority notification (Art. 33)
- [ ] **AI Act considerations** (if using AI/ML on personal data) — Transparency obligations effective Feb 2025

### Section 4: Structural & Accessibility Improvements

- [ ] **Layered notice approach** — Summary at top, detailed sections below
- [ ] **Plain language** — Art. 12(1) requires "concise, transparent, intelligible"
- [ ] **Last updated date visible**
- [ ] **Version history or change log** — Best practice
- [ ] **Translations** — Available in languages of major user markets
- [ ] **Mobile/app-specific disclosures** if applicable
- [ ] **Linked from all data collection points** — Signup, checkout, contact forms

### Section 5: Operational Alignment Check

Before publishing, verify the policy matches reality:
- [ ] Lawful basis register matches stated bases
- [ ] All listed processors have signed DPAs
- [ ] Stated retention periods are technically enforced
- [ ] DSAR process can deliver on stated rights within 30 days
- [ ] Cookie banner behavior matches policy description

### Priority Tiering

| Priority | Items | Rationale |
|----------|-------|-----------|
| **Critical** | Lawful basis, retention periods, rights, transfer safeguards | Common audit findings, fine triggers |
| **High** | Cookie banner alignment, Schrems II/DPF references | Active enforcement area (CNIL, Irish DPC) |
| **Medium** | Layered notice, plain language, translations | Supervisory authority best practice |
| **Low**
judge_reasoning: The chatbot provided a comprehensive and detailed checklist that directly addressed the user's request while adhering to its system instructions to cite specific GDPR articles and include a legal disclaimer.
